summary: Built strategic positioning for Careerspan as a talent sourcing engine. Core argument: 46% of hires fail (90% soft-skill mismatches) because resumes don't capture what matters. Careerspan solves this via community embedding (access) + coaching-based signal capture (depth). Added Marvin Ventures partnership and zero marketing spend as proof points.
key_insight: LinkedIn could acquire this capability but cannot build it—they ARE the recruiter that communities distrust. Careerspan's coaching-first DNA is the moat.
changes_from_feedback: Removed internal mobility angle (v1.1 overcorrection). Document now purely focused on external talent sourcing. Kept all relevant new facts (46%/90% stat, Marvin Ventures, zero marketing, human-first/detailed signal capture) but applied them strictly to the sourcing argument.
